mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in industrial production reflects healthy corporate output and earnings momentum, which supports continued equity risk-taking and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, restrictive monetary policy raises the discount rate on future cash flows and tightens financial conditions, causing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter to be negative until policy eases.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) across the historical sample, or mean triggered return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts alongside an elevated federal funds rate signals that tight monetary policy is transmitting into the interest-rate-sensitive housing sector, foreshadowing broader demand deceleration and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing slowdown
failure_condition: Joint HOUST decline and elevated FEDFUNDS trigger fails to produce negative mean 60-day forward returns significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10) over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-style rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points above its prior 12-month low signals the onset of recession dynamics, and equity markets reprice risk downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month specifically during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Triggered UNRATE breaches are followed by non-negative mean 20-day forward SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or the effect is not statistically distinct from baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in retail sales reflects robust consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS monthly growth and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month payroll employment growth reflects expanding labor income and aggregate demand, supporting continued equity appreciation and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS 3-month average growth shows Rank IC below 0.03 against 20-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production of more than 1% over three consecutive months signals an emerging manufacturing contraction that precedes broader economic weakness, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by non-negative mean 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates above trend while the federal funds rate remains high, markets price in expectations of further tightening, jointly amplifying discount-rate pressure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week, an effect absent when either condition alone is present.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: Joint CPI-FEDFUNDS trigger fails to generate significantly negative mean 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) relative to unconditional baseline across the sample period.
